title: Studies on bis-N-Phenylhydroxamic Acids as Metal-Complexing Ligands : Part II. Adipyl bis-N-Phenylhydroxamic Acid
author: Nripendra Nath Ghosh
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Adipyl bis-N-phenylhydroxamic acid (RH 2  ) forms complex compounds with Cu(II), Fe(lll), Al(III), V(V), and U(VI). The reagent has a tendency of forming tetradenate chelate rings. Compounds as CuR; Fe 2 Ra,2H 2 O; Al 2 R(RH) 2 (OH) 2 ,4H 2 O and VO(OH)R are isolated. Molecules of water in iron and aluminium compounds are lost on heating for two hours at 120° and 108° respectively. Uranyl ion under different conditions forms two different compounds-the mono- and his-compounds UO 2 R,2H 2 O and UO 2 (RH) 2  , 5H 2 O. These compounds become anhydrous by loosing water molecules at 110° and 115° respectively.
steps_raw: Adipyl bis-N-phenylhydroxamic acid (RH 2  ) forms complex compounds with Cu(II), Fe(lll), Al(III), V(V), and U(VI). The reagent has a tendency of forming tetradenate chelate rings. Uranyl ion under different conditions forms two different compounds-the mono- and his-compounds UO 2 R,2H 2 O and UO 2 (RH) 2  , 5H 2 O. These compounds become anhydrous by loosing water molecules at 110° and 115° respectively.
